title: Lesson 155: (Grammar) - want to ... do something; would like to ...; indic...

intro_cards.title: Desire to Act: たい
intro_cards.body:
たい attaches to the verb stem to express desire — 'want to do ~.'

Formation: [verb stem] + たい (conjugates like an い-adjective)

• 日本に行きたい。(I want to go to Japan.)
• 寿司を食べたいです。(I want to eat sushi.)
• 何が飲みたいですか？(What do you want to drink?)

Negative: たくない (don't want to)
• 今日は勉強したくない。(I don't want to study today.)

Past: たかった (wanted to)

Note: たい usually describes your own desire.
For 'she wants to,' use たがっている.
